questions.text: To conduct electricity through Ionic Solids, the ions should be:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: In the context of ionic solids, "excited" usually refers to the state of an electron in an atom or ion that has absorbed energy and moved to a higher energy level. While the concept of electronic excitation is essential in various chemical and physical processes, it is not directly related to the conduction of electricity through ionic solids.

"Energized" generally means that a system or particle has gained energy. In the case of ionic solids, energy is indeed required to initiate the conduction of electricity, but it is not the ions themselves that need to be energized. Instead, the energy typically comes from an external source like an electric field, which allows the ions to move and conduct electricity.

The correct answer is "c) Free." For a material to conduct electricity, it must have mobile charge carriers. In ionic solids, these mobile charge carriers are free ions, which can move under the influence of an electric field. When an ionic solid is dissolved in water or melted, the ions become free and can move, resulting in electrical conductivity.

While the ions in an ionic solid do vibrate and have thermal motion, this random motion is not directly related to the conduction of electricity through the solid. Electrical conductivity in ionic solids primarily depends on the availability of free ions that can move in response to an electric field.

questions.explanation: To conduct energy through Ionic Solids, the ions should be free to move. Ionic compounds conduct electricity as liquids or when in solution since the ions are free to move. Ionic compounds do not conduct electricity in a solid state since the ions are held in place and can not move.

questions.text: Brittleness of ionic solids is due to the fact that ions arrange themselves in a way to _ each other.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic solids are held together by the attraction between positively and negatively charged ions. However, this attraction alone does not explain the brittleness of ionic solids.

This statement is partially true because applying compressive forces to an ionic solid can cause the ions to move closer together, which can increase the strength of the electrostatic forces holding the solid together. However, this does not explain why ionic solids are brittle.

To an ionic compound when a force is applied, like charges in the lattice come together due to the force applied and repel each other, breaking the compound hence ionic compounds are brittle, as like charges repel.
This option is also incorrect. Overlapping of ions would indicate a breakdown in the crystalline structure of an ionic solid, and would not contribute to its brittleness.

questions.explanation: To an ionic compound when a force is applied, like charges in the lattice come together due to the force applied and repel each other, breaking the compound hence ionic compounds are brittle, as like charges repel.

questions.text: The bond length of the Cl-Cl bond in gaseous chlorine is approximately 199pm. In solid iodine, the I-I bond distance is?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is wrong because, in solid iodine, the I-I bond distance is not 143 pm.

In solid iodine, the bond distance between I-I is 271m. The reason is simple. Iodine is two rows down, so, between chlorine and iodine, you have the subshells 4s, 3d, 4p, 5s, 4d, and finally 5p. It is a lot of electrons, which repel each other. The fact that iodine is solid has not much significance: the covalent interactions between iodine atoms are far stronger than the dispersion (London) intermolecular forces; the latter are nevertheless far stronger than in the case of chlorine because a huge number of electrons in a large space means greater polarizability, which makes it possible for iodine to be in a condensed state at room temperature. The reason why it is solid is due to the regular shape of I2 molecules (they are diatomic ones), which makes it easy to pack many molecules to yield a solid structure. Anyway, iodine sublimates very easily.
This option is wrong because, in solid iodine, the I-I bond distance is not 227pm.
This option is wrong because in solid iodine the I-I bond distance is not 166pm.

questions.explanation: Option: B In solid iodine, the bond distance between I-I is 271m. The reason is simple. Iodine is two rows down, so, between chlorine and iodine, you have the subshells 4s, 3d, 4p, 5s, 4d, and finally 5p. It is a lot of electrons, which repel each other. The fact that iodine is solid has not much significance: the covalent interactions between iodine atoms are far stronger than the dispersion (London) intermolecular forces; the latter are nevertheless far stronger than in the case of chlorine because a huge number of electrons in a large space means greater polarizability, which makes it possible for iodine to be in a condensed state at room temperature. The reason why it is solid is due to the regular shape of I2 molecules (they are diatomic ones), which makes it easy to pack many molecules to yield a solid structure. Anyway, iodine sublimates very easily.

options.explanation: Amorphous solid is isotropic, which means that their physical properties are identical in all directions.
Amorphous solids are isotropic so this option is incorrect.

Amorphous solids are isotropic so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: An amorphous solid is isotropic, which means that its physical properties are identical in all directions.

questions.text: Which of the following solids shows hydrogen bonding?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: "Ionic" is incorrect because ionic solids are composed of ions held together by ionic bonds, which are electrostatic attractions between positively and negatively charged ions.

"Metallic" is incorrect because metallic solids are composed of metal atoms held together by metallic bonds, which involve the delocalization of electrons throughout the solid lattice. Metallic bonding does not involve hydrogen bonding.

"Covalent" is incorrect because covalent solids are composed of atoms held together by covalent bonds, which involve the sharing of electrons between atoms. While covalent compounds can have strong bonds, they do not typically exhibit hydrogen bonding.
The correct answer is "Molecular." Solids that exhibit hydrogen bonding are typically molecular solids.

questions.explanation: Ionic solids are crystals held together by ionic bonding between oppositely charged anions and cations. Metals are solids held together by metallic bonding, i.e., electrostatic forces of attraction between positive cations and a negative sea of delocalized electrons. Covalent solids (like diamonds) are held together by covalent bonds between atoms. Molecular solids are held together by intermolecular forces of attraction, including hydrogen bonding and Vander Waals.

questions.text: Crystalline Solids can be converted into amorphous solids by _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Sublimation is the process in which a solid transforms into a gas phase without first melting to form a liquid phase while the conversion of crystalline to Amorphous solid is a solid-to-solid change.
Evaporation is the process by which a liquid turns into a gas. It takes place in liquids.
Many crystalline solids can be changed into amorphous solids by melting and then cooling the molten mass rapidly. In this way, constituent particles do not find time to arrange themselves.
Hammering does not result in an amorphous solid.

questions.explanation: Many crystalline solids can be changed into amorphous solids by melting and then cooling the molten mass rapidly. In this way, constituent particles do not find time to arrange themselves.

questions.text: This is not a molecular solid.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect since sugar consists of sugar molecules associated with hydrogen bonds. It is a molecular solid.
Option B is incorrect since ice contains water molecules associated with hydrogen bonds. It is a molecular solid.
Option C is correct since boron nitride exists as a giant molecular structure and has a structure similar to graphite.
Option D is incorrect since solid iodine is a molecular solid with weak intermolecular forces of attraction. It is a molecular solid.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since boron nitride exists as a giant molecular structure and has a structure similar to graphite. Option A is incorrect since sugar consists of sugar molecules associated with hydrogen bonds. It is a molecular solid. Option B is incorrect since ice contains water molecules associated with hydrogen bonds. It is a molecular solid. Option D is incorrect since solid iodine is a molecular solid with weak intermolecular forces of attraction. It is a molecular solid.

questions.text: Ice is an example of _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Solids in which atoms and molecules are held together by intermolecular forces are called molecular solids. Hydrogen bonded molecular solids contain polar covalent bonds this ice is a familiar example of polar molecular solid.
Non-polar molecular solid is incorrect because water is a polar molecule and ice is a solid form of water.
Ionic solid is incorrect because ice is not made up of ions held together by electrostatic attraction, unlike typical ionic solids such as sodium chloride (NaCl) or magnesium oxide (MgO).
Covalent solid is incorrect because although water molecules are held together by covalent bonds, ice is not a typical covalent solid.

questions.explanation: Solids in which atoms and molecules are held together by intermolecular forces are called molecular solids. Hydrogen bonded molecular solids contain polar covalent bonds this ice is a familiar example of polar molecular solid.

questions.text: The solids which does not possess the regular arrangement of atoms are called as _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: An amorphous solid is any noncrystalline solid in which the atoms and molecules are not organized in a definite lattice pattern. Such solids include glass. Normally solids are closely packed. In comparison, amorphous solids have no such arrangement. Their particles are randomly organized. Crystals have a long-order arrangement of their particles.

Crystalline solids are solids in which the atoms or molecules are arranged in a regular, repeating pattern or crystal lattice. They have a well-defined structure and exhibit characteristic properties such as sharp melting points and the ability to form distinct crystal faces.

Polymorphic solids refer to solids that can exist in different crystal structures or polymorphs. These solids can exhibit multiple crystal structures with different arrangements of atoms or molecules.
Isomorphic solids refer to solids that have the same crystal structure but can be composed of different chemical elements or compounds.

questions.explanation: An amorphous solid is any noncrystalline solid in which the atoms and molecules are not organized in a definite lattice pattern. Such solids include glass. Normally solids are closely packed. In comparison, amorphous solids have no such arrangement. Their particles are randomly organized. Crystals have a long-order arrangement of their particles.

questions.text: Which one the characteristic of Ionic solids?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic compounds have strong intermolecular forces - electrostatic forces, which hold the ions tightly together so they have lower vapor pressures thus this option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect because Ionic compounds cannot conduct electricity when solid, as their ions are held in fixed positions and cannot move.

Ionic solids are composed of cations and anions held together by electrostatic forces. Due to the strength of these interactions, ionic solids tend to be hard, brittle and have high melting points hence this option is incorrect

Ionic compounds dissolve in polar solvents, especially water. This occurs when the positive cation from the ionic solid is attracted to the negative end of the water molecule (oxygen) and the negative anion of the ionic solid is attracted to the positive end of the water molecule (hydrogen).

questions.explanation: Ionic compounds dissolve in polar solvents, especially water. This occurs when the positive cation from the ionic solid is attracted to the negative end of the water molecule (oxygen) and the negative anion of the ionic solid is attracted to the positive end of the water molecule (hydrogen).

questions.text: Molecular solids are held together by weak intermolecular forces called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: a) Van der Waals forces:This option suggests that molecular solids are held together by weak intermolecular forces called Van der Waals forces. Van der Waals forces are relatively weak attractive forces that exist between molecules, particularly in non-polar or weakly polar molecules. These forces arise due to temporary fluctuations in electron distributions, leading to temporary dipoles that induce attractions between neighboring molecules. In molecular solids, the individual molecules are held together by Van der Waals forces.

b) Dipole-dipole interaction:This option states that molecular solids are held together by weak intermolecular forces called dipole-dipole interactions. Dipole-dipole interactions occur in polar molecules and involve the attraction between the positive end of one polar molecule and the negative end of another polar molecule. In molecular solids composed of polar molecules, these dipole-dipole interactions play a role in holding the molecules together.

options.text: Both Options A and B are correct
options.explanation: c) Both Options A and B are correct:This option suggests that both Van der Waals forces (Option a) and dipole-dipole interactions (Option b) are correct and contribute to holding molecular solids together. This statement is accurate. Molecular solids can be composed of both non-polar and polar molecules, and in either case, the weak intermolecular forces (Van der Waals forces in non-polar molecules and dipole-dipole interactions in polar molecules) are essential for their structural stability.

d) Electrostatic forces:This option states that molecular solids are held together by weak intermolecular forces called electrostatic forces. However, this option is not correct. Electrostatic forces generally refer to much stronger interactions, such as ionic or covalent bonds, which are not considered weak intermolecular forces.

questions.explanation: Two types of intermolecular forces hold the particles together in molecular solids: dipole-dipole interactions and van der waal forces.

questions.text: Glass is a/an:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Glass isn’t a pure solid because it lacks the ordered arrangement.

Glass is an amorphous solid / non-crystalline solid in which the atoms or molecules aren’t arranged in a definite lattice pattern. Amorphous solids are also known as super-cooled liquids. Glass has a chemical formula of SiO2.
It isn’t a mixture nor does it contain sodium and calcium.
It isn’t crystalline nor made up of sodium carbonate.
An alloy is a substance that combines more than one metal or mixes metal with a non-metal.

questions.explanation: Glass is sometimes called a supercooled liquid because it does not form a crystalline structure, but instead forms an amorphous solid that allows molecules in the material to continue to move, while it is also true that it’s a homogeneous mixture of sodium and calcium silicates.

questions.text: The crystals formed as a result of vander Waals interactions are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Molecular crystals have Vander Waal forces between them.
Option B is wrong as the covalent crystals have strong covalent bonds between their structure.
Option C is wrong as metallic bonds are bound by strong electrostatic attraction between positively charged metallic ions and delocalized electrons dispersed in the matrix.
Option D is wrong as the ionic crystals are bound by ionic bonding.
Option E is wrong as Option A is correct so none of these cannot be the right option.

questions.explanation: Molecular crystals have Vander Waal forces between them. Covalent crystals have strong covalent bonds between their structures. Ionic crystals are bound by ionic bonding. Metallic bonds are bound by strong electrostatic attraction between positively charged ions and electrons dispersed in the matrix.

questions.text: Ionic solids are characterized by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Ionic solids have strong ionic bonds, which result in a high melting point.
This is incorrect. Ionic solids do not conduct electricity in the solid state because their ions are fixed in place. However, they do conduct electricity when molten or in an aqueous solution.
This is incorrect. Ionic solids typically have low vapour pressure due to strong ionic interactions holding the ions together, making it difficult for them to enter the gas phase.
This is correct. Ionic solids are generally soluble in polar solvents because the solvent molecules can stabilize the ions, overcoming the ionic bonds in the solid.

questions.explanation: Ionic solids are composed of positive and negative ions held together by strong electrostatic forces. This gives them a high melting point and low vapour pressure. They are generally not conductive in the solid state because the ions are immobile, but they conduct electricity when dissolved in water or melted, as the ions are free to move. Ionic solids dissolve in polar solvents due to the interaction between the solvent molecules and the ions, following the principle of 'like dissolves like.'

questions.text: Crystalline part of otherwise amorphous solids are known as:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Allotrope refers to different forms of the same element, each with a unique crystal structure. For instance, carbon's allotropes include diamond and graphite. However, allotropes do not describe crystalline regions within amorphous solids.

Crystallites are small, organized regions within an amorphous solid that possess a regular atomic arrangement. These regions enhance certain properties of the solid, such as melting point and structural order.
Isomorph refers to different substances that share the same crystal structure. For example, NaCl and KCl are isomorphs. This term does not apply to the structure of amorphous solids.

Polymorph describes substances with identical chemical composition but different crystal structures. Ice has several polymorphs, each with distinct structures. It does not describe the crystalline aspects within amorphous solids.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.The crystalline parts of otherwise amorphous solids are known as crystallites.Crystallites are small regions of a solid that have a regular atomic arrangement.They are typically found in amorphous solids,which are solids that do not have a regular atomic arrangement.The presence of crystallites in an amorphous solid gives the solid some of the properties of a crystalline solid,such as a higher melting point and a more ordered structure.

questions.text: Molecular crystals are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Molecular crystals are generally not hard. Molecular crystals are held together by weak intermolecular forces, such as van der Waals forces and dipole-dipole forces. These forces are much weaker than the covalent bonds that hold atoms together in a molecule. As a result, molecular crystals are relatively soft and brittle.
Molecular crystals are never very hard because they lack strong bonds throughout the crystal.This option is incorrect for molecular crystals.

Molecular crystals are soft. Molecular crystals are held together by weak intermolecular forces, such as van der Waals forces and hydrogen bonds. These forces are much weaker than the ionic and covalent bonds that hold together ionic and covalent crystals. As a result, molecular crystals are much softer than ionic and covalent crystals. For example, ice is a molecular crystal that is made up of water molecules. The water molecules in ice are held together by hydrogen bonds. These hydrogen bonds are weak, so ice is a soft solid that can be easily crushed.
Molecular crystals can be made of non-polar molecules (e.g., iodine I₂, dry ice CO₂) or polar molecules (e.g., water H₂O).So, molecular crystals are not always polar.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct.Molecular crystals are soft. Molecular crystals are held together by weak intermolecular forces, such as van der Waals forces and hydrogen bonds. These forces are much weaker than the ionic and covalent bonds that hold together ionic and covalent crystals. As a result, molecular crystals are much softer than ionic and covalent crystals. For example, ice is a molecular crystal that is made up of water molecules. The water molecules in ice are held together by hydrogen bonds. These hydrogen bonds are weak, so ice is a soft solid that can be easily crushed.

questions.text: The nature of binding force in ionic crystals is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: While some materials may exhibit magnetic properties, the primary force holding ionic crystals together is electrostatic attraction between ions. Magnetic forces are associated with the alignment of magnetic dipoles and are not the primary force in ionic crystals.

Ionic crystals are formed through the attraction between oppositely charged ions. While there may be repulsive forces between ions of the same charge, the overall force holding the crystal together is attractive (electrostatic).

Ionic crystals are held together by electrostatic forces of attraction between positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions). This electrostatic force is often referred to as ionic bonding. The strong attraction between opposite charges leads to the formation of a stable crystal lattice structure in ionic compounds.

The forces in ionic crystals are not generally considered weak; in fact, ionic bonds are often quite strong. The strength of the electrostatic attraction between ions in ionic crystals contributes to the stability of their lattice structures.

questions.explanation: An ionic crystal consists of ions bound together by electrostatic attraction. The arrangement of ions in a regular, geometric structure is called a crystal lattice. Examples of such crystals are the alkali halides, which include: potassium fluoride (KF).

questions.text: Atoms in solids are:

options.explanation: Incorrect option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.
Incorrect option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.
Incorrect option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.
Correct option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.

questions.explanation: Atoms in solids are closely packed together, thus solids maintain rigid shapes.

questions.text: Molecular solid may be_?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Neutral crystals refer to crystals that do not have any significant charge imbalances or polarity. These crystals are composed of atoms or molecules with equal distribution of positive and negative charges.

Polar crystals are crystals that have a significant charge imbalance or polarity. These crystals are composed of atoms or molecules with an uneven distribution of positive and negative charges, resulting in a dipole moment.

This option includes both polar and non-polar crystals. It means that some crystals in this category have a significant charge imbalance or polarity (like polar crystals), while others do not (like non-polar crystals)

Non-polar crystals are crystals that do not have any significant charge imbalances or polarity. These crystals are composed of atoms or molecules with an equal distribution of positive and negative charges.

questions.explanation: Molecular solid may be polar or non-polar in nature.An example of polar molecular solid is sucrose and an example of a non-polar molecular solid is silicon dioxide.

questions.text: Amorphous solids turn into glass-like state from rubber-like state, this is called
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: While "transition" is a general term, it doesn't specifically describe the change from a rubber-like state to a glass-like state in amorphous solids. The term is broad and could apply to various changes in states or conditions.

This term is not a commonly used scientific term in the context of the transformation from a rubber-like state to a glass-like state. It doesn't accurately describe the process that occurs in amorphous solids.
The transition from a rubber-like state to a glass-like state in amorphous solids is referred to as the "glass transition."

This option is incorrect because it suggests that all the terms (transition, crack transition, and glass transition) are valid, but "crack transition" is not a recognized term in this context. The appropriate term for the described transition is the "glass transition."

questions.explanation: The glass–liquid transition, or glass transition, is the gradual and reversible transition in amorphous materials (or in amorphous regions within semicrystalline materials) from a hard and relatively brittle "glassy" state into a viscous or rubbery state as the temperature is increased.

questions.text: Molecular solid may be_?

options.explanation: Neutral crystals refer to crystals that do not have any significant charge imbalances or polarity. These crystals are composed of atoms or molecules with equal distribution of positive and negative charges.

Polar crystals are crystals that have a significant charge imbalance or polarity. These crystals are composed of atoms or molecules with an uneven distribution of positive and negative charges, resulting in a dipole moment.

This option includes both polar and non-polar crystals. It means that some crystals in this category have a significant charge imbalance or polarity (like polar crystals), while others do not (like non-polar crystals)

Non-polar crystals are crystals that do not have any significant charge imbalances or polarity. These crystals are composed of atoms or molecules with an equal distribution of positive and negative charges.

questions.text: Which one of them is known as super cooled liquid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Glass is called a supercooled liquid because the molecules in the material are constantly in a state of flux. Glass is an Amorphous solid. Glass does not form a crystalline solid structure because the particles in solids do not move but in the case of glass, it moves. Glass is highly viscous.

Diamond is not a supercooled liquid. It is a crystalline solid that is composed of carbon atoms arranged in a specific crystal lattice structure. While there is some debate about the exact nature of a diamond's atomic structure, it is generally accepted that it is a solid rather than a liquid.

Silica is not a supercooled liquid. Silica is a crystalline solid that is composed of silicon and oxygen atoms arranged in a specific crystal lattice structure. While there is some debate about the exact nature of silica's atomic structure, it is generally accepted that it is a solid rather than a liquid.

Carbon is not a supercooled liquid. Carbon can exist in several different forms, including diamond, graphite, and amorphous carbon. Each of these forms has a distinct atomic structure and physical properties, but none of them are supercooled liquids. Diamond and graphite are both crystalline solids, while amorphous carbon is a non-crystalline solid.

questions.explanation: Glass is called a supercooled liquid because the molecules in the material are constantly in a state of flux. Glass is an Amorphous solid. Glass does not form a crystalline solid structure because the particles in solids do not move but in the case of glass, it moves. Glass is highly viscous.

questions.text: Which of the following is not amorphous solid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Glass is an amorphous solid. It lacks the long-range order found in crystalline solids. Its atomic or molecular structure is disordered and doesn’t have a specific repeating pattern. This lack of ordered arrangement gives glass its unique properties like transparency and the ability to be shaped easily when heated.

Table salt is a crystalline solid. It forms a well-defined crystal lattice structure where sodium (Na+) and chloride (Cl-) ions arrange themselves in a regular, repeating three-dimensional pattern. This ordered arrangement is characteristic of crystalline solids.

Glue can be an amorphous solid. Depending on the type and composition, some glue formulations may solidify into an amorphous structure. Glues often dry and harden without forming a regular crystalline pattern, resulting in an amorphous solid.

Rubber is typically considered an amorphous solid. Its molecular structure lacks a highly ordered arrangement, and instead, it has a tangled and disordered structure. This amorphous nature contributes to rubber's elasticity and flexibility.

questions.explanation: Table salt is a crystalline solid. It forms a well-defined crystal lattice structure where sodium (Na+) and chloride (Cl-) ions arrange themselves in a regular, repeating three-dimensional pattern. This ordered arrangement is characteristic of crystalline solids. Therefore, option b is correct.

questions.text: The crystals formed due to London forces of interaction are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic crystals are formed due to electrostatic forces between charged ions, not London forces. These forces are much stronger and involve the transfer of electrons, unlike the weak London forces.
Molecular crystals consist of individual molecules held together by intermolecular forces, including London dispersion forces. These weak forces are significant in nonpolar molecular crystals.
Covalent crystals are networks of covalent bonds, like in diamond, where atoms share electrons. These bonds are strong and not reliant on the weak London forces.
Metallic crystals involve a lattice of metal cations surrounded by a sea of delocalized electrons. The primary forces here are metallic bonds, not London forces.

questions.explanation: Molecular crystals are formed by individual molecules held together by various types of intermolecular forces, including London dispersion forces. The weak London forces play a significant role in the cohesion of molecular crystals, especially in nonpolar molecules.

questions.text: The best conductors of electricity are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic solids consist of ions held together by electrostatic forces. They conduct electricity only when melted or dissolved in water, as the ions are free to move in these states.
Metallic solids have a 'sea of electrons' that are free to move, making them excellent conductors of electricity in their solid state.
Molecular solids are composed of molecules held together by van der Waals forces. They generally do not conduct electricity as they lack free electrons or ions.
Covalent solids have atoms connected by covalent bonds throughout the structure. Most covalent solids do not conduct electricity, except for materials like graphite, which has a special structure.

questions.explanation: Metallic solids are the best conductors of electricity due to their unique structure that features a 'sea of delocalized electrons.' These electrons are not bound to any specific atom, allowing them to move freely throughout the solid, facilitating electrical conduction. In contrast, ionic solids require a liquid or dissolved state to conduct electricity, as the ions are fixed in place in the solid form. Molecular solids do not conduct electricity because they lack free-moving charged particles. Covalent solids, while having strong bonds, do not usually allow for free electron movement, with some exceptions like graphite.

questions.text: Dry ice is an example of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Dry ice is a molecular solid because it is composed of CO₂ molecules held together by intermolecular forces like Van der Waals forces, which are characteristic of molecular solids.
Metallic solids consist of metal atoms held together by a sea of delocalized electrons, which give them unique properties such as conductivity and malleability. This is not applicable to dry ice.
Covalent solids have atoms bonded together by covalent bonds in a network, resulting in hard and high-melting-point materials, like diamond. Dry ice does not fit this description.
Ionic solids are formed by the electrostatic attraction between cations and anions, creating a lattice structure. Dry ice, being molecular in nature, does not belong to this category.
Plastics are synthetic materials made from polymers, not naturally occurring molecular solids like dry ice. Plastics are versatile and used in many applications, unlike dry ice.

questions.explanation: In dry ice, the CO₂ molecules are held together in the solid by weak intermolecular forces (not by strong covalent or ionic bonds).Because of these weak forces, dry ice sublimates easily at −78.5°C instead of melting into a liquid under normal atmospheric pressure.

questions.text: Amorphous solids turns into glass like state from rubber like state, this is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is quite general and doesn't specify the particular transformation being discussed. "Transition" can refer to various changes or shifts without specifying the specific change from a rubbery to a glass-like state in amorphous solids.

This option mentions a transition related to cracks, which doesn't accurately describe the change from a rubbery state to a glass-like state in amorphous materials. The glass transition is not about cracking but rather the transformation of the material's physical properties.

The process where amorphous solids transform from a rubbery or viscous state to a glassy state without any crystallization is known as the "glass transition." This transition is specifically referred to as the glass transition temperature (Tg).
This option is incorrect as only option c is correct.

questions.explanation: The glass–liquid transition, or glass transition, is the gradual and reversible transition in amorphous materials (or in amorphous regions within semicrystalline materials) from a hard and relatively brittle "glassy" state into a viscous or rubbery state as the temperature is increased.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true for Amorphous solids?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Amorphous solids lack symmetry because their atomic arrangement is irregular, unlike crystalline solids which have a symmetrical, orderly pattern.
Amorphous solids are isotropic, meaning they have uniform properties in all directions due to their irregular atomic arrangement, lacking long-range order.
Anisotropic materials, like crystalline solids, have direction-dependent properties, which is not the case for amorphous solids.

options.text: They cleave along a particular direction
options.explanation: Cleavage along specific directions is characteristic of crystalline solids due to their ordered structure. Amorphous solids break irregularly because of their random atomic arrangement.
Amorphous solids do not have a definite shape or form since their atoms are not arranged in a fixed, repeating pattern.

questions.explanation: Amorphous solids are isotropic because their lack of long-range order results in uniform physical properties in all directions. This is in contrast to crystalline solids, which are anisotropic due to their orderly atomic structure. Amorphous solids do not possess symmetry and cannot cleave along particular planes because their particles are randomly arranged. Additionally, they do not exhibit a definite shape as their molecular structure does not form a regular lattice.

questions.text: Carbon dioxide is an example of?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The statement is inaccurate as carbon dioxide (CO2) does not exist as ions linked by ionic bonds. Instead, it is a covalent compound comprised of two oxygen atoms that are covalently bonded to a central carbon atom.

The assertion is inaccurate, as metallic solids are characterized by a lattice structure comprised of metal atoms held together by metallic bonding. In contrast, carbon dioxide is classified as a molecular compound that features covalent bonds, rather than being classified as a metallic solid.

A molecular solid is defined as a solid comprised of discrete molecules that are held together by van der Waals forces. An example of such a solid is carbon dioxide (CO2), which exhibits relatively weak London Dispersion Forces. This characterization highlights the distinct properties associated with molecular solids.

Covalent Solid: This classification is not accurate, as covalent solids are typically characterized by a network of atoms interconnected by strong covalent bonds, exemplified by diamond, a crystalline form of carbon. In contrast, carbon dioxide is composed of discrete molecules rather than forming a continuous covalent network.

questions.explanation: A molecular solid is made up of discrete molecules held together by van der Waals forces. CO2 exemplifies this type of solid due to its London Dispersion Forces, making option C correct.

questions.text: Chloride ions in NaCl are placed at the corner of a regular:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Incorrect, a pentagon does not have the required symmetry for the arrangement of chloride ions in NaCl.

In the crystal structure of NaCl:It has a face-centered cubic (FCC) lattice.Chloride ions (Cl⁻) form the FCC unit cell: they are located at the corners and the face centers of the cube.Sodium ions (Na⁺) occupy the octahedral voids created by the chloride ions.
Sodium ions (Na⁺) occupy the octahedral voids created by the chloride ions.
The chloride ions in NaCl are placed at the corners of a regular cube.

questions.explanation: Chloride ions are placed in a CCP type of arrangement. It contains chloride ions at the corner and the center of the cube. 6 chloride ions surround one sodium atom. So, The location of Na+ and Cl– is such that each Na+ is surrounded by six Cl– ions placed at the corners of a regular octahedron.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true for Amorphous solids?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Amorphous solids lack symmetry because their atomic arrangement is irregular, unlike crystalline solids which have a symmetrical, orderly pattern.
Amorphous solids are isotropic, meaning they have uniform properties in all directions due to their irregular atomic arrangement, lacking long-range order.
Anisotropic materials, like crystalline solids, have direction-dependent properties, which is not the case for amorphous solids.

options.text: They cleave along a particular direction
options.explanation: Cleavage along specific directions is characteristic of crystalline solids due to their ordered structure. Amorphous solids break irregularly because of their random atomic arrangement.
Amorphous solids do not have a definite shape or form since their atoms are not arranged in a fixed, repeating pattern.

questions.explanation: Amorphous solids are isotropic because their lack of long-range order results in uniform physical properties in all directions. This is in contrast to crystalline solids, which are anisotropic due to their orderly atomic structure. Amorphous solids do not possess symmetry and cannot cleave along particular planes because their particles are randomly arranged. Additionally, they do not exhibit a definite shape as their molecular structure does not form a regular lattice.

questions.text: Which of the following substances has a giant covalent structure?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Iodine crystal is composed of diatomic molecules (I2) held together by weak van der Waals forces. It does not have a giant covalent structure.
Solid carbon dioxide, also known as dry ice, consists of discrete CO2 molecules held together by weak van der Waals forces. It is not a giant covalent structure; rather, it is a molecular solid.

Silica, also known as silicon dioxide (SiO2), has a giant covalent structure. In a giant covalent structure, atoms are bonded together by strong covalent bonds throughout the entire structure, forming a network. Silica is an example of a network covalent solid, where each silicon atom is covalently bonded to four oxygen atoms in a tetrahedral arrangement, and this network extends throughout the entire crystal. The strong covalent bonds result in a high melting point and a hard, crystalline structure.

White phosphorus (P4) exists as P4 tetrahedra, where each phosphorus atom is covalently bonded to three other phosphorus atoms. However, it does not form a giant covalent structure like silica. White phosphorus has a molecular structure at the P4 level.

questions.explanation: Silica tends to form long-chain covalent structures such as carbon so it has a giant covalent structure. Diamond and graphite (forms of carbon) and silicon dioxide (silica) are examples of giant covalent structures (lattices) of atoms. All the atoms in these structures are linked to other atoms by strong covalent bonds and so they have very high melting points.

questions.text: Glass is a/an:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Glass isn’t a pure solid because it lacks the ordered arrangement.

Glass is an amorphous solid / non-crystalline solid in which the atoms or molecules aren’t arranged in a definite lattice pattern. Amorphous solids are also known as super-cooled liquids. Glass has a chemical formula of SiO2.
It isn’t a mixture nor does it contain sodium and calcium.
It isn’t crystalline nor made up of sodium carbonate.
An alloy is a substance that combines more than one metal or mixes metal with a non-metal.

questions.explanation: Glass is sometimes called a supercooled liquid because it does not form a crystalline structure, but instead forms an amorphous solid that allows molecules in the material to continue to move, while it is also true that it’s a homogeneous mixture of sodium and calcium silicates.

questions.text: The nature of binding force in ionic crystals is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: While some materials may exhibit magnetic properties, the primary force holding ionic crystals together is electrostatic attraction between ions. Magnetic forces are associated with the alignment of magnetic dipoles and are not the primary force in ionic crystals.

Ionic crystals are formed through the attraction between oppositely charged ions. While there may be repulsive forces between ions of the same charge, the overall force holding the crystal together is attractive (electrostatic).

Ionic crystals are held together by electrostatic forces of attraction between positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions). This electrostatic force is often referred to as ionic bonding. The strong attraction between opposite charges leads to the formation of a stable crystal lattice structure in ionic compounds.

The forces in ionic crystals are not generally considered weak; in fact, ionic bonds are often quite strong. The strength of the electrostatic attraction between ions in ionic crystals contributes to the stability of their lattice structures.

questions.explanation: An ionic crystal consists of ions bound together by electrostatic attraction. The arrangement of ions in a regular, geometric structure is called a crystal lattice. Examples of such crystals are the alkali halides, which include: potassium fluoride (KF).

questions.text: Atoms in solids are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Incorrect option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.
Incorrect option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.
Incorrect option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.
Correct option. The particles present in solid substances are very close to each other and they are tightly packed.

questions.explanation: Atoms in solids are closely packed together, thus solids maintain rigid shapes.

questions.text: The stability of ionic crystal depends principally on:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: High electron affinity of anion forming species
options.explanation: High electron affinity indicates the tendency of an atom to accept an electron, forming an anion. While it is important for ion formation, it is not the primary factor determining the stability of an ionic crystal.

Lattice energy is the energy released when gaseous ions form an ionic solid. It is directly related to the strength of the electrostatic forces between ions in the lattice, which determines the stability of the ionic crystal. Greater lattice energy implies a more stable crystal.

options.text: Low ionization energy of cation forming species
options.explanation: Low ionization energy refers to the ease with which an atom can lose an electron to form a cation. This facilitates ion formation but does not directly impact the stability of the ionic crystal once formed.

options.text: Low heat of sublimation of cation forming solid
options.explanation: Heat of sublimation is the energy required to convert a solid to gas. While it reflects the strength of intermolecular forces within a solid, it is not relevant to the stability of the ionic crystal lattice.

questions.explanation: The stability of an ionic crystal is primarily determined by its lattice energy, which is the energy released when ions in the gaseous state form an ionic solid. This energy reflects the strength of the electrostatic forces between oppositely charged ions in the crystal lattice. The higher the lattice energy, the more stable the ionic crystal is. Option B correctly identifies lattice energy as the key factor contributing to the stability of ionic crystals.Option A, while addressing electron affinity, pertains to ion formation rather than crystal stability. Option C discusses ionization energy, relevant to cation formation but not to the stability of the formed crystal. Option D involves heat of sublimation, which is unrelated to ionic crystal stability.

questions.text: The shape of the crystal in which it usually grows is called its _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it is not a correct definition of size.
This option is incorrect because it is not a correct definition of capacity.
This option is correct because it is a definition of habit. Habit of a crystal is defined as the shape of the crystal in which it usually grows.
This option is incorrect because it is not a correct definition of property.

questions.explanation: The correct option is C. The Habit of a crystal is defined as the shape of the crystal in which it usually grows.

questions.text: Structure of ice is similar to which of the following?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Liquid water does not have a regular repeating pattern, and the arrangement of water molecules is more disordered compared to the solid state.

The structure of ice is similar to the structure of DIAMOND. Ice and diamond are both examples of crystalline structures. In ice, water molecules are arranged in a regular, repeating pattern forming a crystal lattice. Each water molecule is bonded to four neighboring water molecules through hydrogen bonds. This arrangement gives ice its solid and stable structure. Similarly, a diamond is also a crystalline structure, where carbon atoms are arranged in a three-dimensional lattice. Each carbon atom is covalently bonded to four other carbon atoms, creating a very rigid and stable structure.
Graphite is composed of carbon atoms arranged in layers.
Sucrose is a molecular compound with a different structure altogether.

questions.explanation: The correct option is B. The structure of ice is similar to the structure of DIAMOND. Ice and diamond are both examples of crystalline structures. In ice, water molecules are arranged in a regular, repeating pattern forming a crystal lattice. Each water molecule is bonded to four neighboring water molecules through hydrogen bonds. This arrangement gives ice its solid and stable structure. Similarly, a diamond is also a crystalline structure, where carbon atoms are arranged in a three-dimensional lattice. Each carbon atom is covalently bonded to four other carbon atoms, creating a very rigid and stable structure.

questions.text: Which of the following solids is isotropic?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because Ionic solids are composed of cations and anions held together by electrostatic forces. Due to the strength of these interactions, ionic solids tend to be hard, brittle, and have high melting points.

This option is incorrect because Molecular solids are composed of discrete molecules held together by intermolecular forces. Because these interactions are relatively weak, molecular solids tend to be soft and have low to moderate melting points.
This option is correct because Amorphous solids are isotropic. That is, they exhibit uniform properties in all directions.

This option is incorrect because Metallic solids are materials that consist of metal atoms exclusively, giving them a unique set of properties. They are held together by a special type of bond called metallic bonding. This occurs when a lattice of positively charged metal ions is surrounded by a sea of delocalized electrons.

questions.explanation: The correct option is C. Amorphous solids are isotropic. That is, they exhibit uniform properties in all directions. The thermal and electrical conductivities, coefficient of thermal expansion, and refractive index of an amorphous solid have the same value in whatever direction the properties are measured.

questions.text: NaCl and CsF have same geometry because they have the same _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionisation energy refers to the amount of energy required to remove an electron from an atom or a positive ion. It is a measure of how strongly an atom holds onto its electrons.When an electron is removed from an atom, it creates a positively charged ion. The ionisation energy is the energy needed to overcome the attractive forces between the positively charged nucleus and the negatively charged electron, allowing the electron to be removed.Ionisation energy values vary for different elements and atoms. Generally, elements with higher atomic numbers tend to have higher ionisation energies because the electrons are held more tightly due to increased nuclear charge. Additionally, the ionisation energy generally increases as you move across a period in the periodic table from left to right.Ionisation energy is an important property as it helps us understand the reactivity and behavior of elements. Elements with low ionisation energies tend to be more reactive, as they can easily lose electrons and form positive ions.

NaCl and CsF have the same geometry because they have the same ionic radius ratio. The ionic radius ratio is the ratio of the radius of the cation to the radius of the anion in an ionic compound. In both NaCl and CsF, the cation is smaller than the anion, but the ratio of their radii is the same. As a result, both compounds adopt the same crystal structure, which is face-centered cubic. The other properties mentioned, such as ionization energy, electron affinity, and bond length, may be different for NaCl and CsF because they have different cations and anions. However, these properties do not directly affect the geometry of the crystal structure.

Electron affinity refers to the energy change that occurs when an atom or molecule gains an electron to form a negative ion. It is a measure of how much an atom or molecule "wants" or attracts an additional electron.When an atom or molecule gains an electron, it goes from a neutral state to a negatively charged state. The electron affinity is the energy released or absorbed during this process. If the electron affinity is positive, it means that energy is released when an electron is added, indicating a favorable electron gain. On the other hand, if the electron affinity is negative, it means that energy is required to add an electron, indicating an unfavorable electron gain.The electron affinity of an element or molecule depends on various factors such as its atomic or molecular structure, the number of electrons already present, and the electronic configuration. Elements with higher electron affinities tend to have a greater tendency to gain electrons and form negative ions.

Incorrect option!NaCl and CsF have the same geometry because they have the same ionic radius ratio. The ionic radius ratio is the ratio of the radius of the cation to the radius of the anion in an ionic compound. In both NaCl and CsF, the cation is smaller than the anion, but the ratio of their radii is the same. As a result, both compounds adopt the same crystal structure, which is face-centered cubic. The other properties mentioned, such as ionization energy, electron affinity, and bond length, may be different for NaCl and CsF because they have different cations and anions. However, these properties do not directly affect the geometry of the crystal structure.

questions.explanation: NaCl and CsF have the same geometry because they have the same ionic radius ratio. The ionic radius ratio is the ratio of the radius of the cation to the radius of the anion in an ionic compound. In both NaCl and CsF, the cation is smaller than the anion, but the ratio of their radii is the same. As a result, both compounds adopt the same crystal structure, which is face-centered cubic. The other properties mentioned, such as ionization energy, electron affinity, and bond length, may be different for NaCl and CsF because they have different cations and anions. However, these properties do not directly affect the geometry of the crystal structure

questions.text: Crystals are obtained by the saturated solution through?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Sedimentation is the process of particles settling to the bottom of a liquid. This process is used to separate solids from liquids, but it does not produce crystals.
Drying is the process of removing water or other solvents from a substance. This process can be used to obtain crystals, but it is not the only step involved.
Heating can be used to dissolve solids in solvents, but it does not produce crystals.

Crystals are obtained from a saturated solution through cooling. When a saturated solution is cooled, the solute molecules can no longer stay dissolved in the solvent and they start to form crystals. The crystals will continue to grow until the solution is no longer saturated.

questions.explanation: Crystals are obtained from a saturated solution through cooling. When a saturated solution is cooled, the solute molecules can no longer stay dissolved in the solvent and they start to form crystals. The crystals will continue to grow until the solution is no longer saturated.

questions.text: Crystalline solids are made up of _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because not all crystalline solids are made up of atoms. Crystalline solids can consist of various types of particles, including atoms, molecules, or ions. Examples of crystalline solids made up of atoms include diamond (composed of carbon atoms) and metallic crystals (composed of metal atoms).

This option is incorrect because not all crystalline solids are made up of molecules. Crystalline solids can contain molecules as their building blocks, such as sugar (composed of individual sugar molecules) or ice (composed of water molecules). However, many other crystalline solids, like sodium chloride (NaCl) or calcium carbonate (CaCO3), are not composed of discrete molecules but instead consist of ions or atoms.

This option is incorrect because not all crystalline solids are made up of ions. Some crystalline solids, like molecular crystals, are composed of molecules rather than ions. However, many ionic compounds form crystalline solids, such as sodium chloride (NaCl) or potassium nitrate (KNO3), where the crystal lattice is made up of positively and negatively charged ions held together by ionic bonds.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: The correct answer is "all of these," because crystalline solids can be made up of a variety of constituents, including atoms, molecules, and ions. Crystalline solids are characterized by their highly ordered and repeating three-dimensional arrangements of constituent particles. These particles can be atoms, molecules, or ions depending on the specific substance.

questions.explanation: Crystalline solids are a three-dimensional collection of individual atoms, ions, or whole molecules organized in repeating patterns.

questions.text: The existence of an element in more than one crystalline form is called
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isomerism refers to the phenomenon in which two or more compounds have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements.
Allotropy is the existence of two or more different forms of an element in the same physical state.
Anisotropy refers to the property of a material or a system that exhibits different physical or mechanical properties when measured along different axis or directions.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: Only option b is correct, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Allotropy, or allotropism, is the property of some elements to exist in two or more different forms in the same physical state, known as allotropes of the elements. Allotropes are different structural modifications of an element the atoms of the element are bonded together differently. Examples are diamond and graphite, which are both allotropic forms of carbon.

questions.text: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes to other is called _ temperature:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Solution and Colloids

options.explanation: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes into another is called the transition temperature or phase transition temperature. This process is commonly observed in materials undergoing a phase transition, such as from a solid to another solid polymorphic form. The specific terms for these transitions can vary depending on the nature of the substance, for example, it could be called a melting point, freezing point, or transition temperature.

questions.explanation: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes into another is called the transition temperature or phase transition temperature. This process is commonly observed in materials undergoing a phase transition, such as from a solid to another solid polymorphic form. The specific terms for these transitions can vary depending on the nature of the substance, for example, it could be called a melting point, freezing point, or transition temperature.

questions.text: Melting point of crystalline solids are
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Option A is correct since crystalline solids have a sharp melting point which is fixed. This feature is a characteristic of crystalline solids.
Option B is incorrect since it shows the characteristics of amorphous solids.
Option C is incorrect since it shows that the crystalline solid is impure.
Option D is incorrect since it states none of these which is not the case.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct since crystalline solids have a sharp melting point which is fixed. This feature is a characteristic of crystalline solids. Option B is incorrect since it shows the characteristics of amorphous solids. Option C is incorrect since it shows that the crystalline solid is impure. Option D is incorrect since it states none of these which is not the case.

questions.text: Structure of a crystal is changed due to ---------
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Incorrect option. Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

Incorrect option. Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

Incorrect option. Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since the presence of impurity due to its different electronic structure produces disturbance in the conduction electron distribution, distortion of the crystal lattice, and polarization of electronic charge. These facts change the various electronic properties of metallic solids significantly.

questions.text: 'Anisotropy' is a property of crystal based on its -------
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Chemical properties refer to how a substance interacts with other substances to form new chemical compounds.

A lattice refers to a regular and repeating three-dimensional pattern of atoms, ions, or molecules that make up a crystal structure. The lattice structure can be thought of as a three-dimensional network of points, where each point represents an atom, ion, or molecule.
Physical properties refer to characteristics that can be observed or measured without changing the substance's chemical identity, such as color, melting point, boiling point, etc.
Density is a physical property that describes how much mass is contained in a given volume of a substance.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since some crystals show variation in physical properties depending upon their direction of cleavage. Such properties are called 'Anisotropic Properties'. For example: refractive index, and electric and thermal conductivities.

questions.text: Which one of the following can conduct electricity in a solid state?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Diamond does not conduct electricity.
In graphite each carbon is bonded to three other carbon atoms hence leaving behind a free electron. Due to the presence of this one delocalised electron, graphite is a good conductor of electricity .

Fingernails do not conduct electricity and thus cannot be employed to enter information on a conductive touch screen. Users with long fingernails cannot easily touch the touch screen with their finger.
Iodine is a nonmetal and therefore, it is an insulator that is, relatively, a poor conductor of electricity and heat .

questions.explanation: Option A: Diamond does not conduct electricity.Option B: In graphite, each carbon is bonded to three other carbon atoms hence leaving behind a free electron. Due to the presence of this one delocalized electron, graphite is a good conductor of electricity.Option C: fingernails do not conduct electricity and thus cannot be employed to enter information on a conductive touch screen. Users with long fingernails cannot easily touch the touch screen with their fingerOption D: Iodine is a nonmetal and therefore, it is an insulator that is, relatively, a poor conductor of electricity and heat.

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect since cooling affects the decrease in temperature.
Option B is incorrect since heating causes the bonds between the atoms to relax.
Addition of impurity in a crystal alters it structure, thus physical properties are also changed.

Option D is incorrect since Option C is the right answer.
